value_chain_position: Industrial software / PLM platform layer — sells the systems-of-record (Windchill PLM, Creo CAD, Codebeamer ALM, ServiceMax SLM, Onshape) that govern the full product lifecycle and the structured product-data foundation customers need before they can apply AI. A genuine software toll-taker in the design-to-manufacture-to-service chain; load-bearing for connected-factory and AI-driven engineering, though it is a top-5 PLM/CAD vendor (alongside Siemens, Dassault, Autodesk), not a sole gatekeeper.
gm_trajectory: EXPANDING — gross margin ~83.8% and rising, holding the low-80s%+ across FY23->FY24->FY25 on +12-19% revenue; expanding GM on growing revenue = real pricing power / switching-cost moat, the moat lie-detector PASSES.
bull_thesis: PTC is a high-quality industrial-software compounder hiding behind a flattered-then-feared print, now on sale ~50% off its 52-week high ($113.68 vs $219.69 high; near the $108.50 low). The moat is proven by the numbers, not the rhetoric: gross margin ~83.8% and EXPANDING, net margin 26.8%, FCF margin 31.3%, FCF 3-yr CAGR +27.9%, and a 100% (7/7) EPS-beat rate. Windchill/Creo/Codebeamer are sticky systems-of-record with high switching costs — a PLM rip-and-replace is a multi-year, multi-million-dollar enterprise project, which is exactly why GM expands instead of compressing. The secular theme is durable and web-verified: the PLM software market is ~$50B in 2026 growing ~8% CAGR (cloud sub-segment ~11%), and the AI overlay is a genuine tailwind, not an air-pocket — customers must modernize their product-data foundation BEFORE AI works, and PTC owns that foundation (Garrett Motion, largest-ever Codebeamer and Onshape deals are real competitive displacements of Siemens/Dassault/Autodesk). Management executed a clean strategic refocus: sold the lower-quality Kepware/ThingWorx IoT businesses to TPG (closed Mar-13-2026, ~$375M net after-tax) and concentrated 100% on the 83%-GM core. Capital allocation is now a coiled spring — deleveraged from 1.5x to <1x, then pivoting to ~$1.225-1.325B of buybacks in FY26 plus a fresh $2B authorization through FY28, retiring stock from ~121M to ~115-116M shares AT a depressed price (highly accretive). On ~$950M normalized FY26 FCF and a 16x P/FCF, the business is being valued like a no-growth industrial when it is an 8-9% ARR grower with elite economics and an embedded-AI-agent optionality (systems-of-record evolving to 'systems of action'). Conditions that must hold: (1) deferred-ARR-under-contract converts into the guided Q4'26 net-new-ARR step-up and FY27/FY28 ramp; (2) GM stays in the low-80s; (3) macro/tariff/auto-OEM paralysis continues to ease rather than re-intensify.
bear_thesis: The 'disruptor' label oversells a decelerating high-single-digit compounder. ARR growth fell every quarter in the window — 10% -> 9.3% -> 8.5% — and FY26 is guided to 7.5-9.5%, BELOW the old 10%+ aspiration; this is not an accelerating secular ramp, it is a maturing software annuity. The headline +19.2% revenue and the TTM diluted EPS of 10.49 (+270% latest-Q) are MISLEADING: revenue is flattered by longer-duration renewals and license timing (management admitted Q2'26 that 'renewals with longer duration' drove revenue/EPS above range), and the EPS spike is juiced by the one-time divestiture gain-on-sale plus a shrinking buyback-driven share count — not clean operating leverage. Forward consensus EPS growth is only +7.7%, and margins already sit ~1.78 sigma above their historical peak, leaving little room for further margin-led EPS upside; future growth must come from ARR, which is decelerating. The entire re-acceleration case rests on 'record deferred ARR' that ramps in FY27/FY28 — a promise, not a delivered number — and analysts have explicit prior burns ('PTSD... deferred ARR that didn't pan out,' Wolfe, Q1'26 and Q2'26), with FY26 dangerously back-loaded into a Q4 step-up. AI monetization is pure vision: no dollars, no pricing, no attach metrics ('we'll detail these as they take shape'). PTC is also NOT a sole gatekeeper — Siemens Teamcenter and Dassault 3DEXPERIENCE are larger and can route around it in aero/auto; the top three (Dassault/Autodesk/Siemens) hold >65% of CAD. Smart-money refuses to validate: 0 open-market insider buys vs 16 sells (66,970 sh), smart-money score 32/100, analyst-target velocity -7%, stock making fresh 52-week lows. The DCF and EPV models scream caution (DCF fair value 79, MoS -44%; EPV -100%) — only the Buffett/Graham/IV15 models support the high fair value, and those lean on the flattered earnings. What confirms the bear: a Q4'26 net-new-ARR step-up that fails to materialize, or any GM erosion signaling AI/cloud-mix commoditization.
sop_bull: $165-180/sh. Favorable parts on the FOCUSED post-divestiture company (~$950M normalized FY26 FCF, ~115.5M diluted shares). Value the high-quality recurring software franchise (Windchill/Creo/Codebeamer/ServiceMax/Onshape — ~88% recurring) at a premium software multiple in line with Dassault/Autodesk: ~24x normalized FCF on $950M = ~$22.8B equity. Plus AI/'systems-of-action' agent optionality (embedded engineering agents general-purpose models can't reach) worth a modest ~$1.0B premium. Gross EV ~ $23.8B; subtract net DEBT of ~$0.94B (2026-03-31). Equity ~$22.9B / 115.5M sh = ~$198/sh raw; haircut for back-loaded deferred-ARR execution risk to a defensible ~$165-180/sh. Cross-check: analyst consensus PT $186.88 and the screener Buffett FV $247 bracket the top of this range; the buyback shrinking the count toward ~115M is per-share accretive.
sop_bear: $95-110/sh, ASSUMING THE PLM/AI THEME PAUSES 12-18 MONTHS (deferred ARR slips, net-new-ARR step-up disappoints, AI monetization stays vaporware). Re-value the same recurring base at a no-growth industrial-software multiple ~13-14x on a stressed ~$900M FCF = ~$12.2B equity value; the AI optionality goes to ~$0 in a pause. Gross EV ~$12.2B; subtract net debt $0.94B -> equity ~$11.3B / 115.5M sh = ~$98/sh. Corroborated by the DCF fair value of 79 (MoS -44%) and EPV -100% — the cash-flow-only models land in the high-$70s to high-$90s, defining the downside floor. Bear per-share ~ $95-110.
sop_breakdown: Focused recurring software franchise valued at ~18x normalized FY26 FCF of ~$950M = ~$17.1B equity (a blended multiple between the bear's no-growth 13-14x and the bull's premium 24x, reflecting a durable but only 8-9% grower with elite 83.8% expanding GM and 31% FCF margins). AI/agent optionality carried at a conservative ~$0.4B (real but unmonetized) -> gross EV ~$17.5B. Subtract net DEBT $0.94B (2026-03-31; a liability, NOT net cash) -> equity ~$16.6B. Divide by ~115.5M diluted shares (post-buyback) = ~$144; round the base toward $135 to absorb the back-loaded deferred-ARR realization risk and the flattered-headline discount. Sanity vs peers: ~18x FCF / ~mid-20s P/E on clean earnings is a discount to Dassault and roughly in line with a decelerating Autodesk — defensible for a top-5 PLM toll-taker, NOT a stretch.
risk_reward: FAVORABLE but quality-priced. Downside-to-break ~$98 (bear SoP / DCF floor) = ~14% below the $113.68 price. Upside-to-fair ~$135 base (+19%) and ~$165-180 bull (+45-58%). Asymmetry ~1.4-3.5x to the upside; the buyback at the lows and elite cash conversion cushion the floor, but the upside is gated on unproven deferred-ARR re-acceleration, so this is a quality-compounder-on-sale, not a coiled special-sit.
catalyst_status: FIRED — the load-bearing corporate catalyst (Kepware/ThingWorx divestiture to TPG) COMPLETED Mar-13/16-2026 (~$523M cash at close, ~$375M net after-tax, redeployed into a $375M ASR); the re-rate from the deal itself is spent. Per moderator mandate this is a PROFITABLE-DISRUPTOR (theme-durability) judgment, not a catalyst trade, so catalyst_status does NOT drive the verdict. Theme-load-bearing facts web-verified as of Jun-2026: PLM market ~$50B/2026 at ~8% CAGR (cloud ~11%); PTC top-5 with 83.8% expanding GM; Q2'26 (May-6-2026) ARR +8.5% CC at high end of guide, FCF +14% above range, FY26 ARR reaffirmed 7.5-9.5%; the forward variable (deferred-ARR -> Q4'26 net-new-ARR step-up + FY27/FY28 ramp) remains PENDING and unproven.
peer_comps_note: theme_map true_competitors: ADSK, Dassault (DSY.PA), Siemens (SIE.DE), Hexagon (HEXA-B.ST), ABB, Rockwell (ROK). PTC is the clearest pure-play industrial-software toll-taker in the set (88% recurring, 83.8% GM) vs the more hardware/automation-mixed ABB/Rockwell. Dassault and Autodesk are the right multiple anchors (both ~20-30x FCF historically); Siemens DI and Dassault are LARGER in PLM and can route around PTC in aero/auto, capping the premium. The chosen ~18x base FCF multiple sits at a discount to Dassault/Autodesk and a premium to ABB/Rockwell — appropriate for a higher-margin but slower-growing (8-9% ARR) software peer. Multiple is sanity-checked, not a stretch.
consensus_delta: Two-sided mispricing. The STREET that drove the stock to a fresh 52-week low (~$113.68, -50% from $219.69) is over-extrapolating the ARR deceleration (10%->8.5%) and the tariff/auto-OEM-paralysis scare as terminal, pricing PTC at ~16x P/FCF — a no-growth-industrial multiple for an 8-9% ARR grower with 83.8% EXPANDING gross margin and 31% FCF margins. That is the bull's expectations-arb: the market is mistaking deceleration for decay in a switching-cost annuity. BUT the symmetric error is the analyst/Buffett-model bull camp (consensus PT $186.88, Buffett FV $247): it capitalizes the FLATTERED headline — +19.2% revenue and TTM EPS 10.49 are inflated by longer-duration renewals, license timing, the one-time divestiture gain, and a buyback-shrunk share count, NOT clean 19% operating growth. The honest center is an ~8-9% ARR compounder with elite economics worth ~$135 base — meaningfully above the $113 price (street too bearish) but well below the $187 PT and $247 Buffett FV (those camps too bullish on flattered earnings). The specific street assumption that is wrong: that the deferred-ARR-driven Q4'26 step-up and FY27/FY28 re-acceleration are already de-risked — they are asserted, not delivered, and the DCF/EPV models (FV 79 / -100%) flag exactly this overhang.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 mo) risks are execution and macro, not solvency — net debt is only $0.94B against ~$950M annual FCF and leverage <1x, so there is NO maturity wall or cash-burn hump. The real valley is the BACK-LOADED FY26: management guided net-new ARR to a Q3'26 range of only $40-55M and a 'more significant step-up in Q4,' meaning the FY26 ARR bridge depends on a single Q4 (Sep-2026 quarter, reported ~Nov-2026) conversion of deferred ARR. If that step-up disappoints — the exact scenario analysts have prior 'PTSD' over — the stock re-rates down toward the bear SoP (~$98). Secondary headwinds: tariff/auto-OEM capex paralysis (eased but not gone — energy-price/war strains cited Q2'26), FX, and divestiture-stub-period noise muddying YoY rev/EPS comps (Kepware/ThingWorx in FY25 but not full FY26, NOT recast). Margins sitting 1.78 sigma above peak leave little buffer if mix shifts. None of these are fatal, but each is a flush risk that could clear the decks at a better entry.
positioning_washout: Not a dividend/yield-base washout (PTC pays no dividend; holders are growth/quality funds, not income). The mechanical pressure is the reverse: passive/index reweighting after the ~50% drawdown plus growth-fund de-risking on the ARR-deceleration narrative is what MANUFACTURED the 52-week low. Insider behavior confirms no internal capitulation-buying: 0 open-market buys vs 16 sells (66,970 sh), smart-money score 32/100, analyst-target velocity -7%. The cleanest washout signal to wait for is a disappointing Q4'26 net-new-ARR step-up (Nov-2026) that forces the last momentum holders out and lets the ~$1.3B/yr buyback absorb the supply at a true bottom — that is the capitulation event that turns a 'B' into an 'A'.
forcing_function: Two dated forcing functions. (1) Q3 FY26 earnings (~late-Jul/early-Aug-2026): tests whether net-new ARR hits the guided $40-55M and whether the back-half pipeline is real — a beat starts re-rating the too-bearish street. (2) THE decisive one — Q4/FY26 earnings (~Nov-2026): the 'significant step-up' quarter where the record deferred-ARR balance must finally convert into net-new ARR and validate (or break) the FY27 $950M-FCF base. That print is the binary that either confirms the 8-9% durable compounder thesis (re-rate toward ~$135-165) or confirms the bear (deferred ARR slipped again -> ~$98). The divestiture catalyst is already FIRED, so the re-rate now hinges entirely on this deferred-ARR conversion proof, not a corporate event.
moderator_conclusion: PTC is a genuine PROFITABLE SECULAR DISRUPTOR on the metrics that matter for a theme-durability judgment, but it is a QUALITY COMPOUNDER, not a coiled special-situation — hence verdict B, conviction 3, with a divergent value_conviction of 4. THEME DURABILITY: real and multi-year, web-verified — PLM software is a ~$50B/2026 market growing ~8% CAGR (cloud ~11%), and the AI overlay is a structural tailwind (customers must modernize the product-data foundation PTC owns before AI works), NOT a hyperscaler-capex air-pocket; this theme does not roll over on a single capex cycle. LOAD-BEARING POSITION (4/5): PTC owns sticky systems-of-record with high switching costs and is winning competitive displacements (Garrett Motion, largest-ever Codebeamer/Onshape), but it is a top-5 vendor — Siemens Teamcenter and Dassault 3DEXPERIENCE are larger and can route around it in aero/auto, so it is load-bearing, not a sole gatekeeper. MOAT / GM LIE-DETECTOR: PASSES decisively — gross margin ~83.8% and EXPANDING on growing revenue is textbook pricing power, the opposite of commoditization; 31% FCF margins and a 100% EPS-beat rate corroborate. REINVESTMENT economics are good (high-incremental-margin recurring software, ~8% TAM CAGR with cloud/AI headroom) though incremental ROIC is bounded by an 8-9% top line and ROIC of ~10%. WHY ONLY B/3 ON CONVICTION: the catalyst is FIRED (divestiture closed Mar-2026, re-rate spent) and the re-acceleration that would justify aggressive entry rests on an UNPROVEN, back-loaded deferred-ARR conversion (Q4'26) that analysts have prior burns on; insiders are net sellers, smart money is absent (32/100), and the headline growth (+19% rev, +270% Q-EPS) is flattered by renewals timing, the divestiture gain, and buybacks rather than clean operating leverage. WHY value_conviction 4 (DIVERGES UP): stripped of the catalyst overlay, the value case is strong — an elite-economics software annuity trading at ~16x FCF / ~50% off its high, a reconciled SoP fair value of ~$135 (+19%) against a defensible ~$98 downside (1.4-3.5x asymmetry), funded by ~$1.3B/yr of accretive buybacks at the lows; the forensic quality is high (credibility 4/5, no customer concentration, clean balance sheet). NET: own the quality on weakness and accumulate, but WAIT FOR THE Q4'26 NET-NEW-ARR STEP-UP (Nov-2026) as the specific flush/proof event before sizing up — buy the compounder, do not pay up today for an AI/deferred-ARR re-acceleration that is still a promise.
